zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne de Brabant Duchess of Brabant and Limbou於1322年6月24日出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇時，她的父親Hertog Jan III van Brabant22歲，母親Comtesse Marie d'Évreux20歲。她於1334年與Count William II of Hainault Avesnes在荷蘭布拉班特布波瑟爾結婚。她去世於1406年11月1日在比利時布拉班特布波瑟爾享年84歲，埋葬在比利時布拉班特布波瑟爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jeanne de Brabant Duchess of Brabant and Limbou于1322年6月24日出生在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇时，她的父亲Hertog Jan III van Brabant22岁，母亲Comtesse Marie d'Évreux20岁。她于1334年与Count William II of Hainault Avesnes在荷兰布拉班特布波瑟爾结婚。她去世于1406年11月1日在比利时布拉班特布波瑟爾享年84岁，埋葬在比利时布拉班特布波瑟爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Brabant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Aristide, Marcel, Normand, Ovila, Yvan.
French, Walloon, Flemish, and German: habitational name from Brabant, a province in the Low Countries.

English: cognate of 1 above, from Old French Brabant, Middle English Braban, Brabayn, used to denote both the region and an inhabitant of it. See also the synonymous Brabazon . In medieval England, Brabans were typically linen weavers and cloth merchants. The final syllable of Braban, when weakly stressed, was often altered to -ben, -bin, or -bon, and also to -bam. The additional -t or -d in post-medieval Brabant, Braband , etc. is probably excrescent, rather than a relic of the Old French form.

variants.name: Brabender
meanings: German (mainly Westphalia): habitational name for someone from Brabant (see   Brabant  ), perhaps applied also as a nickname for a merchant of Brabantine cloth.

variants.name: Brabazon
meanings: English: from Old French Brabançon, Anglo-Norman French Brabanzon, Middle English Brab(a)son ‘Brabanter’. This was originally an ethnic name for a native of the duchy of Brabant (now in north Belgium). Medieval Brabant was widely known for two exports, its brutal mercenary soldiers and its linen cloth. In the 13th century parts of France were devastated by marauding gangs of armed Brabanters, and brabançon came to be used generically to denote a mercenary, hired assassin, or robber. These originated in Brabant and Flanders, but in the course of time accepted recruits from anywhere. The true source of the surname is the presence in many English (and Irish) towns of Brabant cloth merchants.

variants.name: Bremner
meanings: Scottish: habitational name for someone from Brabant in the Low Countries, from Older Scots Brebner, Brabanare ‘native or inhabitant of Brabant’ (see Brabant and compare with the synonymous Brabazon ). Weavers from Brabant settled early at Aberdeen and on the east coast of Scotland.
German: habitational name for someone from the city of Bremen. This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Brabham
meanings: English: variant of Brabant , altered by association with English habitational names ending in -ham.

variants.name: Braband
meanings: German and English: variant of   Brabant  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne de Brabant Duchess of Brabant and Limbou was born on 24 June 1322, in Holy Roman Empire, her father, Hertog Jan III van Brabant, was 22 and her mother, Comtesse Marie d'Évreux, was 20. She married Count William II of Hainault Avesnes in 1334, in Brussels, Brabant, Netherlands. She died on 1 November 1406, in Brussels, Brabant, Belgium, at the age of 84, and was buried in Brussels, Brabant, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne de Brabant Duchess of Brabant and Limbou est née le 24 juin 1322, à Saint-Empire romain, son père, Hertog Jan III van Brabant, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Comtesse Marie d'Évreux, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Count William II of Hainault Avesnes en 1334, à Bruxelles, Brabant, Pays-Bas. Elle est décédée le 1 novembre 1406, à Bruxelles, Brabant, Belgique, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterrée à Bruxelles, Brabant, Belgique.

Juni 1322 in Heiliges Römisches Reich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hertog Jan III van Brabant 22 und ihre Mutter Comtesse Marie d'Évreux 20. Sie hat 1334 in Brüssel, Brabant, Niederlande Count William II of Hainault Avesnes geheiratet. Sie starb am 1. November 1406 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Brüssel, Brabant, Belgien und wurde in Brüssel, Brabant, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Brabant Duchess of Brabant and Limbou è nata il il 24 giugno 1322 nel Heiliges Römisches Reich, sua padre, Hertog Jan III van Brabant, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Comtesse Marie d'Évreux, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Count William II of Hainault Avesnes nel 1334, nel Brussel, Brabant, Paesi Bassi. Morì il 1 novembre 1406, nel Brussel, Brabante, Belgio, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolta a Brussel, Brabante, Belgio.

ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne de Brabant Duchess of Brabant and Limbou(이)가 1322년 6월 24일 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hertog Jan III van Brabant(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Comtesse Marie d'Évreux(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1334년 네덜란드 브라반트 브루셀에서 Count William II of Hainault Avesnes(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1406년 11월 1일 벨기에 브라반트 브루셀에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 브라반트 브루셀에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de Brabant Duchess of Brabant and Limbou nasceu em 24 de junho de 1322, em Heiliges Römisches Reich, seu pai, Hertog Jan III van Brabant, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Comtesse Marie d'Évreux, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Count William II of Hainault Avesnes em 1334, em Bruxelas, Brabant, Países Baixos. Ela faleceu em 1 de novembro de 1406, em Bruxelas, Brabante, Bélgica, com 84 anos, e foi sepultada em Bruxelas, Brabante, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne de Brabant Duchess of Brabant and Limbou родилась 24 июня 1322 г. в местности хилигесь рoмищесь раич, её отцу по имени Hertog Jan III van Brabant было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Comtesse Marie d'Évreux, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Count William II of Hainault Avesnes в 1334 г. в местности брассел, брабант, Нидерланды. Она умерла 1 ноября 1406 г. в местности брассел, брабант, Бельгия в возрасте 84 лет и была похоронена в местности брассел, брабант, Бельгия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jeanne de Brabant Duchess of Brabant and Limbou nació el 24 de junio de 1322, en Heiliges Römisches Reich, su papá, Hertog Jan III van Brabant, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Comtesse Marie d'Évreux, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Count William II of Hainault Avesnes en 1334, en Brussel, Brabant, Países Bajos. Murió el 1 de noviembre de 1406, en Brussel, Brabante, Bélgica, a la edad de 84 años, y fue sepultada en Brussel, Brabante, Bélgica.
